data.description:
## Why
Leftover VCS conflict markers in committed source are unambiguous, polyglot, and zero-FP — deterministic
check territory, not an LLM pass. This is the #5 reclassification: a detector, not prompt text.

## What
Add a deterministic DET criterion (exec: DET) that flags VCS conflict markers (<<<<<<<, =======, >>>>>>>)
and obvious encoding corruption (NUL bytes) on ADDED lines of changed files. Polyglot regex; advisory /
fail-open (blocking_enabled false). Reuses the Engine B detector consumer pattern
(engine_b.scan -> apply_failclosed) and criteria_routing.json routing — no new engine code. Explicitly
drops the fuzzy dso parts (truncation stubs, pasted-non-code-text) as too FP-prone for a zero-config check.

## Scope
A builtin detector rule + criteria_routing.json entry; src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py wiring
if needed. Diff-scoped match; whole-scan abstain handled by existing fail-open logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Conflict markers on added lines produce an advisory finding; clean diffs do not.
- [ ] Detector is polyglot (language-agnostic) and fail-open; no new BLOCK source.
- [ ] Recall+no-fire eval passes (marker present vs. a legitimate '=======' inside a string/heredoc).
data.title: Deterministic conflict-marker detector (DET criterion)
